question.text: Which option position carries theoretically unlimited risk?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A short call writer must deliver at the strike price regardless of how high the market price goes. Since prices can rise indefinitely, the risk is theoretically unlimited. **The Trap:** Long call and long put have risk limited to premium. Short put has maximum loss of Strike - Premium (underlying can only go to zero). **Cross-Exam Context:** Risk profiles of all four basic option positions are tested in NISM Equity and Interest Rate Derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is why naked call writing is considered the riskiest option strategy. Short put risk is large but bounded (underlying cannot go below zero).
